name: 11-Week Build · 47-Mile Race (Aug 1)
description:
Custom 11-week cycling plan: weight loss + 47-mile road race on Saturday August 1, 2026.

6 rides/week. Sunday is rest (family day). Saturday is the long-ride anchor (builds to ~40 mi). Tuesday & Thursday are the two hard days. Monday, Wednesday, Friday are TRULY easy.

Targets use Heart Rate Reserve zones (matches your Whoop):
  Z1 Recovery: 116-139 bpm
  Z2 Endurance: 140-151 bpm
  Z3 Tempo: 152-163 bpm
  Z4 Threshold: 164-174 bpm
  Z5 VO2 max: 175-186 bpm

Set Cadence's HR zones to match these exact numbers (Settings → Heart Rate).
Set plan start date to Thursday June 4, 2026.
author: Claude

workout.name: Tempo Intervals 3 × 5 min
workout.description: Tempo intro. 3 × 5 min in Z3 (152-163) with 3 min easy recovery. Comfortably hard. Short sentences only.
workout.author: Claude

subSteps.name: 5 min Z3
subSteps.instructions: Z3 tempo. HR in Z3 (152-163). Comfortably hard.

subSteps.name: 3 min recovery
subSteps.instructions: Easy spin. HR back to Z1.

workout.name: Easy Endurance Z2 (60 min)
workout.description: Friday easy ride. RULE: average HR must stay in Z2 (140-151). If you drift up to Z3, you rode too hard. Practice eating: snack at 30-min mark.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Z2 First Half
steps.instructions: Z2. HR in low Z2 (140-145). Stay easy on purpose. Eat at end of this block.

steps.name: Z2 Second Half
steps.instructions: Continue Z2. HR avg should stay in Z2 (140-151).

workout.name: Long Endurance Ride (105 min · ~22 mi)
workout.description: Saturday long ride · target ~22 miles. Mostly Z2 (140-151). Let HR drift to Z3 on hills, recover at top. Eat every 25-30 min. Drink every 15-20 min.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Z2 Block 1
steps.instructions: Z2 endurance. HR 140-151. Eat at end of this block.

steps.name: Z2 Block 2
steps.instructions: Continue Z2. Drink. Eat again at end.

workout.name: Easy Recovery Spin (30 min)
workout.description: Pure recovery. Stay in Z1 (116-139) the whole time. Should feel insultingly slow. Light gears, 85-95 RPM. Flush legs, not train.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Easy Spin Z1
steps.instructions: HR in Z1 (116-139). Conversational. Light gears. Feel insultingly slow.

workout.name: Tempo Intervals 3 × 6 min
workout.description: Tempo build. 3 × 6 min Z3 with 3 min easy. Hold steady HR across each interval.
workout.author: Claude

workout.name: Easy Endurance Z2 (45 min)
workout.description: Z2 endurance. HR in Z2 (140-151) the whole way. Conversational pace. Pure aerobic base.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Z2 Endurance
steps.instructions: Z2. HR in Z2 (140-151). Conversational. If you can't talk in full sentences, slow down.

workout.description: Threshold intro. 4 × 4 min Z4 (164-174) with 4 min easy. Hard but sustainable. One-word answers only.

workout.name: Threshold HR Test (55 min)
workout.description: FTP-style test for HR-only athletes. 20 min WU · 20 min ALL-OUT (max sustainable, EVEN pacing) · 15 min CD. Note your avg HR for the 20-min effort. Threshold HR = avg × 0.95.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: 20-MIN ALL-OUT TEST
steps.instructions: 20 MIN ALL-OUT. EVEN pacing — hardest you can hold for full 20. Don't blow up early. Note avg HR.

steps.name: Long Cool-down
steps.instructions: 15 min easy cool-down. Done. Record your avg HR for the 20-min effort.

workout.name: Tempo Intervals 2 × 20 min (Peak)
workout.description: Peak tempo. 2 × 20 min Z3 with 7 min easy. Sustained efforts at race intensity.
workout.author: Claude

subSteps.name: 20 min Z3
subSteps.instructions: Z3 tempo. HR in Z3 (152-163). Long sustained.

workout.author: Claude

workout.name: Threshold Intervals 4 × 8 min (Peak)

workout.author: Claude

subSteps.name: 8 min Z4
subSteps.instructions: Z4. HR in Z4 (164-174). Hold steady, don't blow up early.

workout.name: Easy Endurance Z2 (45 min)
workout.description: Z2 endurance. HR in Z2 (140-151) the whole way. Conversational pace. Pure aerobic base.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Z2 Endurance
steps.instructions: Z2. HR in Z2 (140-151). Conversational. If you can't talk in full sentences, slow down.

workout.name: Race Simulation (3h 10m · ~40 mi)
workout.description: Peak race-pace simulation. ~3 hours. Z2 base with 2 × 15 min Z3 efforts in the middle. Full nutrition rehearsal: eat what you'll eat race day, wear race kit, use race bottles. Find problems NOW.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Z2 Long Block
steps.instructions: Z2 to the end. Eat at 25 and 50 min.

workout.name: Easy Recovery Spin (30 min)
workout.description: Pure recovery. Stay in Z1 (116-139) the whole time. Should feel insultingly slow. Light gears, 85-95 RPM. Flush legs, not train.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Easy Spin Z1
steps.instructions: HR in Z1 (116-139). Conversational. Light gears. Feel insultingly slow.

workout.name: Race Openers (50 min)
workout.description: Tuesday before race. Z2 with 4 × 2 min Z4 efforts to keep legs sharp without adding fatigue.
workout.author: Claude

subSteps.name: 2 min Z4
subSteps.instructions: 2 min Z4. HR in Z4 (164-174). Sharp.

steps.name: Cool-down
steps.instructions: 10 min cool-down. Legs sharp, not tired.

workout.name: Pre-Race Spin (30 min)
workout.description: Thursday before race. Easy Z2 with 3 × 1 min race-pace efforts. Loose legs, no fatigue.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Cool-down
steps.instructions: Spin out. Save it for Saturday.

workout.name: 🏁 RACE DAY — 47 Miles
workout.description: RACE DAY. 47 miles. Pacing: First 5 mi deliberately easy. Miles 5-40 solid Z3/low Z4. Miles 40-47 empty the tank. Two bottles with carbs+electrolytes. Eat 60-80g carbs/hour (3-4 gels/bars). Start drinking mile 5, eating mile 10. EXECUTE.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Warm-up
steps.instructions: 15-min warm-up with 3 × 30-sec pickups. Open the legs.

steps.name: First 5 mi — HOLD BACK
steps.instructions: Deliberately too easy. Adrenaline lying to you. HR stays under top of Z3.

steps.name: Race effort (miles 5-40)
steps.instructions: Solid Z3 / low Z4. Hard but sustainable. One-word answers. Eat at 30/60/90/120/150 min.

steps.name: Final push (miles 40-47)
steps.instructions: Last 7 miles. Empty the tank if you have legs.

steps.name: Cool-down
steps.instructions: Easy spin if possible. Eat protein+carbs within 60 min.

workout.name: Post-Race Easy Spin (30 min)
workout.description: Post-race recovery. Very easy Z1 / low Z2. No pushing whatsoever.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Easy Recovery
steps.instructions: Easy spin. HR in Z1 (116-139). Recovery only.

workout.name: Easy Endurance Z2 (45 min)
workout.description: Z2 endurance. HR in Z2 (140-151) the whole way. Conversational pace. Pure aerobic base.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Z2 Endurance
steps.instructions: Z2. HR in Z2 (140-151). Conversational. If you can't talk in full sentences, slow down.

workout.name: Post-Race Easy Endurance (75 min)
workout.description: Saturday after race. Z2 ride with no agenda. Just enjoy being on the bike.
workout.author: Claude

steps.name: Z2 No Agenda
steps.instructions: Z2 endurance. Conversational. Pick a route you love.
